summary: Ronacher explains his recent drop in activity as a mix of university frustration and a desire to explore beyond Python and web development. He plans to keep maintaining his projects but expects low activity while he learns new areas. He outlines specific plans for Werkzeug, Jinja, and Zine, including delayed Python 3 support and release timing constraints. He expresses dissatisfaction with Python 3’s trajectory and is waiting on WSGI decisions before porting Werkzeug. He calls for more contributors to sustain his projects, noting that solo maintenance limits progress. The post closes with a pragmatic outlook: reduced focus but continued stewardship and a hope for steadier releases in 2010.
key_points: University constraints and broadening interests are reducing his open-source output for a while.
Werkzeug will release soon but won’t be ported to Python 3 yet; some components are being deprecated or postponed.
Jinja’s Python 3 support is near but blocked by compiler scoping edge cases; he seeks failing templates to fix them.
Zine will get a release but lacks a clear future due to being a one-person, niche Python project.
He actively seeks contributors and potential new owners to keep projects healthy.
money_quotes: University turned out to be boring and stressful at the same time and is the main limiting factor of my productivity.
So my Python / Webdev activity will most likely stay as low as it currently is for a while.
This is also my biggest grief at the moment. I am totally unhappy how Python is developing currently and especially with the implementation of Python 3 and the plans for it.
Until then however, I do not plan to port Werkzeug to Python 3.
All that would not be an issue if I could generate bytecode directly, but that unfortunately is not portable and does not work on the appengine at all.
Zine is still a one man project and because it is built in Python it is mostly interesting for other Python developers and as such missing its market.
Right now I'm pretty much alone on these and working alone means that in times where I have few time to spare, the amount of new features, improvements and everything declines.
key_insight: He is stepping back from intense Python/web work to broaden his skills, but plans to keep projects alive and is seeking community help to sustain them.
